blocks:
سُورة النجم مكّيّة

Surah An-Najm is Makkan

And it is narrated from Ibn Abbas that except for one verse, which was revealed in Medina: الَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبائِرَ الْإِثْمِ ** (those who avoid the major sins), the verse [was revealed in Medina]. And from Al-Hasan, he said: It is Medinan. The number of its verses is sixty-two verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** وَالنَّجْمِ إِذا هَوى ** (By the star when it sets) He swore by the star when it falls.

(٢) ما ضَلَّ صاحِبُكُمْ ** (your companion has neither gone astray) Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله has not deviated from the straight path, وَما غَوى** (nor has he erred) nor has he believed in falsehood. The intent is to negate what they attribute to him.

(٣) وَما يَنْطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوى. (nor does he speak out of [personal] desire.)

(٤)** إِنْ هُوَ ** (It is nothing) Meaning, that which He speaks with, ** إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحى ** (but a revelation revealed [to him]), which Allah reveals to Him.

In al-Majalis, narrated from Ibn Abbas, he said: We prayed the last Isha prayer one night with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. When He concluded the prayer, He turned His face towards us and then said: "A star will fall from the sky at the rising of the dawn, and it will drop into the house of one of you. Whoever has that star fall into his house, he is my successor (وصيّى), my Caliph (خليفتي), and the Imam after me." When the dawn approached, every one of us sat in his house awaiting the star to fall into his house. The most hopeful person among the people regarding this was my father, Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib. When the dawn broke, the star fell from the air and dropped into the house of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Ali, by Him Who sent me with prophethood, the succession (الوصيّة), the Caliphate (الخلافة), and the Imamate (الإمامة) after me have become incumbent upon you." Then the hypocrites, Abdullah ibn Ubayy and his companions, said: "Muhammad has certainly gone astray due to the love of his cousin and has erred, and he does not speak concerning his affair except out of desire (الهوى)." So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed: وَالنَّجْمِ إِذا هَوى ** (By the star when it sets). The Mighty and Majestic says: "By the Creator of the star إِذا هَوى ما ضَلَّ صاحِبُكُمْ ** (when it sets, your companion has neither gone astray)," meaning, in the love of Ali ibn Abi Talib, وَما غَوى (nor has he erred), وَما يَنْطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوى (nor does he speak out of [personal] desire), meaning, concerning his affair, إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحى. (It is nothing but a revelation revealed [to him]).

And from Al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them): [A narration] close to it.

And Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Rida (peace be upon him): That **النَّجْمِ ** (the star) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله.

And from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: ما ضَلَ ** (He has neither gone astray) concerning Ali (peace be upon him), وَما غَوى ** (nor has he erred), وَما يَنْطِقُ ** (nor does he speak) concerning him عَنِ الْهَوى ** (out of [personal] desire). And what He said concerning him was nothing but the revelation (الوحي) which was revealed to Him.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Regarding وَالنَّجْمِ إِذا هَوى ** (By the star when it sets), He said: He swore by the grave of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله when He was taken [in death]: ما ضَلَّ صاحِبُكُمْ ** (your companion has neither gone astray) by preferring His Household (أهل بيته), وَما غَوى وَما يَنْطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوى ** (nor has he erred, nor does he speak out of [personal] desire). He says: He does not speak of the merit of the Household (أهل بيت) out of His own desire (هواه), and that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحى.** (It is nothing but a revelation revealed [to him]).

And in al-Majalis, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the contentment (رضى) of people cannot be possessed, and their tongues cannot be controlled. And how can you be safe from that from which the Prophets of Allah, His Messengers, and the Proofs (حجج) of Allah were not safe? Did they not attribute to our Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله that He speaks out of desire (الهوى) concerning His cousin Ali (peace be upon him), until Allah refuted them and said: وَما يَنْطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوى إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحى. (nor does he speak out of [personal] desire. It is nothing but a revelation revealed [to him]).

(٥) عَلَّمَهُ شَدِيدُ الْقُوى ** (5) He was taught by the one mighty in power, It was said that this means Gabriel, while Al-Qummi means Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. (٦) ذُو مِرَّةٍ ** (6) the possessor of strength, Possessor of soundness (حصافة) in his intellect and opinion. فَاسْتَوى ** then he settled, So he became straight/upright (فاستقام). It was said that this means Gabriel became straight/settled upon the true form which Allah created him upon. For it was narrated: None of the prophets saw him in his form except Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, once in the heaven and once on the earth. And Al-Qummi means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And narrated from Al-Rida عليه السلام: Allah did not send a prophet except that He was the possessor of a pure black مرّة (مرّة سوداء صافية).

(٧) وَهُوَ بِالْأُفُقِ الْأَعْلى ** (7) while He was in the highest horizon. It was said: It means Gabriel (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. (٨) ثُمَّ دَنا ** (8) Then he drew near It was said: It means Gabriel from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from his Lord. فَتَدَلَّى ** and came closer, So he increased in nearness from Him. This is its interpretation. And the origin of al-Tadalli (التدلّي) is letting down/hanging down with attachment (استرسال مع تعلّق). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said that it was only revealed as فَتَدانى.

In al-Ilal (العلل), narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding: **فَتَدَلَّى ** He said: "Do not read it like this. Read: ثُمَّ دَنا فَتَدانى."

(٩) فَكانَ قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ ** (9) (and he was) within two bow-lengths the measure of them (the two bows). Al-Qummi said: He was from Allah as the distance between the grip of the bow and the tip of the siyyah (السية). I say: The explanation and interpretation of that will follow. أَوْ أَدْنى ** (or nearer) He said: Rather, nearer than that. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The first of creation to precede others to (بلى) [Yes] was the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. That is because he was the closest of creation to Allah, and he was in the place where Gabriel said to him when he was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِهِ) to the heaven: "Advance, O Muhammad, for you have trodden a place that neither a proximate angel nor a sent prophet has trodden." Were it not that his spirit (رُوحُهُ) and soul (نَفْسُهُ) were from that place, he would not have been able to reach it. And he was from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, as He said: قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى (within two bow-lengths or nearer), meaning: rather, nearer. And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He was asked whether Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is described by place. He said: Allah is exalted far above that. It was asked: Then why was His Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِهِ) to the heaven? He said: To show him the dominion (مَلَكُوت) of the heavens and the wonders of His making and the marvels of His creation therein. It was asked: Then what about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ثُمَّ دَنا فَتَدَلَّى فَكانَ قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى (Then he drew near and came down, till he was two bow-lengths away, or nearer)? He said: That was the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He drew near to the veils of Light (حُجُب النّور) and saw the dominion (مَلَكُوت) of the heavens. Then he came down (تَدَلَّى) and looked from beneath him at the dominion (مَلَكُوت) of the earth, until he thought that in proximity to the earth, he was like قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى. (within two bow-lengths or nearer). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِهِ) and was from his Lord like two bow-lengths or nearer, a veil from His veils was lifted for him. And in al-Amali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: When I was ascended (عُرِجَ بِي) to the heaven and drew near to my Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, until there was between Him and me قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى ** (within two bow-lengths or nearer), He said to me: "O Muhammad, whom among creation do you love?" I said: "O Lord, Ali." He said: "Then look, O Muhammad." So I looked to my left, and behold, there was Ali ibn Abi Talib. And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), he said: I am the son of him who ascended (عَلا) and rose high (اسْتَعْلَى), passed the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary (سِدْرَة الْمُنْتَهَى), and was from his Lord قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى. (within two bow-lengths or nearer). And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about His saying دَنا فَتَدَلَّى ** (drew near and came down). He said: This is a dialect (لُغَة) among Quraysh; when a man among them intends to say, "I have heard," he says, "I have come down (قَدْ تَدَلَّيْتُ)." And al-tadalli (التدلّي) is understanding (الفهم). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِهِ) from the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) to the Farthest Mosque (المسجد الأقصى), a journey of a month, and He was ascended (عُرِجَ بِهِ) through the dominion (مَلَكُوت) of the heavens, a journey of fifty thousand years, in less than a third of a night, until He reached the Leg of the Throne (ساق العرش). So He drew near with knowledge (بالعلم) and came down (فَتَدَلَّى). A green cushion (رَفْرَف) from Paradise drew near to Him, and the Light covered His sight. He saw the greatness of his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, with His heart (فُؤَادِهِ) and did not see it with His eye. Thus, He was like two bow-lengths between Him and Him, or nearer. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how many times the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was ascended (عُرِجَ بِهِ). He said: Twice. Gabriel stopped him at a station (مَوْقِف) and said to him: "Stay in your place, O Muhammad, for you have stood in a station that neither an angel nor a prophet has ever stood in. Indeed, your Lord is praying (يُصَلِّي)." He said: "O Gabriel, and how does He pray?" He said: "He says: 'All-Glorious (سُبُّوح), All-Holy (قُدُّوس), I am the Lord of the angels and the Spirit (الرُّوح). My Mercy has preceded My Wrath.'" So he said: "O Allah, Your pardon (عَفْوُك), Your pardon." He said: And he was as Allah said: قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى ** (within two bow-lengths or nearer). It was asked: What is قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى ? He said: The distance between its siyyah (سيتها) and its tip (رأسها). He said: There was a veil (حِجَاب) between them that glittered (يَتَلَأْلَأ) and fluttered (يَخْفِق). I do not know except that he said it was emerald (زَبَرْجَد). He looked through something like the eye of a needle (سَمّ الابرة) at whatever Allah willed of the Light of Greatness (نُور العَظْمَة). Then Allah Almighty said: "O Muhammad." He said: "Here I am, my Lord (لَبَّيْك رَبّي)." He said: "Who is for your nation (لِأُمَّتِك) after you?" He said: "Allah knows best." He said: "Ali ibn Abi Talib, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), the Master of the Muslims (سيّد المسلمين), and the Leader of the Bright-Foreheaded (قائد الغرّ المحجّلين)." Then al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: By Allah, the guardianship (وِلايَة) of Ali did not come from the earth, but rather it came from the heaven, spoken directly (مُشَافَهَة). I say: There is no contradiction (تَنَافِي) among these narrations, and all of them originated from the source of knowledge (مَعْدِن الْعِلْم) according to the capacities of the understanding of those addressed. The siyyah (سِيَة) of the bow—with the kasrah on the undotted letter (المهملة) before the lightened doubled letter below (المثنّاة التحتانية المخفّفة)—is what is bent back from its two ends. This is a representation (تَمْثِيل) of the spiritual, non-physical measure (المقدار المعنوي الرّوحاني) by a physical, formal measure (المقدر الصوري الجسماني), and the proximity of rank (القرب المكانتي) by spatial nearness (الدنوّ المكاني). Allah is exalted far above what the anthropomorphists (المشبّهون) say, with great exaltation. The Imam interpreted the measure of two bows (القوسين) as the measure of the two bent ends of a single bow (القوس الواحد), as if He made each of them a separate bow. Thus, the measure of the sum of the two bows is the measure of one bow, which is called the ring-bow (قوس الحلقة), and this is before it is prepared for shooting, for then it is semi-circular, and the circle is divided by what is called the arc (القوس). In expressing this meaning with such a phrase, there is a subtle indication (إشارة لطيفة) that the traveler (السائر) in this journey descended from Him, the Glorified, and ascended to Him, and that the upward movement (الحركة الصعودية) was reflective (انعطافيّة), and that it did not occur over the exact distance of the descent, but over another distance, as has been verified in its proper place. Thus, his journey was from Allah, to Allah, in Allah, by Allah, and with Allah, blessed is Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. The veil (الحجاب) that was between them is the veil of humanity (حجاب البشريّة). It glittered (يَتَلَأْلَأ) because of its immersion (انغماسه) in the Light of the Lord Almighty, with fluttering (بِخَفْق), meaning with agitation and movement (اضطراب وتحرّك). That was because he was almost completely annihilated from himself (يَفْنَى عَنْ نَفْسِهِ بِالْكُلِّيَّة) in the Light of Lights (نُور الأنوار) due to the overwhelming power of Majesty (سَطَوَات الْجَلال) and his complete attraction (بِشَرَاشِرِهِ) to the presence of the Most High Holiness (جَنَاب الْقُدْس الْمُتَعَال). This is what is meant by the spiritual descent (التدلّي المعنوي). Describing the veil as emerald (الزّبرجد) is an allusion (كِنَايَة) to His presence, because the Divine Light (النور الإلهي), which resembles the color white in representation, had been mixed with human darkness (ظُلْمَة بَشَرِيَّة), so it became tuyar (تُيَرَا), meaning it was as if it were green, the color of emerald (الزّبرجد). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, only asked him about his successor (خَلِيفَتِهِ) because the matter of the Imamate (الإمامة) was of concern to him, and it was in his heart to leave behind his successor among them when he departed from them. Allah knew that from him, and that is why He asked him about it. Since the successor was designated (مُتَعَيِّناً) by Allah and by His Messenger, Allah said what He said and described him with attributes that no one else could attain. In this hadith are profound secrets (أسرار غامضة) that the hands of our limited understandings (أَيْدِي أَفْهَامِنَا الْخَافِضَة) cannot reach. The more we strive to reveal them, the more we increase their concealment, especially regarding the meaning of the prayer (صَلَاة) of Allah, the Glorified, and His Prophet's request for pardon (العَفْو) in response to it. Nevertheless, we have alluded to a glimpse of that in our book called al-Wafi (الوافى) in the commentary on this hadith. And from Allah is the assistance in understanding its secrets.

(10) فَأَوْحى إِلى عَبْدِهِ ما أَوْحى (10) Then He revealed to His servant what He revealed.

In the ambiguity of what was revealed (الموحى به), there is a magnification of it. Al-Qummi said: He revealed it verbally (مشافهة).

And in al-Ihtijaj, in the narration previously mentioned: So, among what He revealed to him is the Ayah which is in Surat al-Baqarah, the Almighty's saying:

لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِنْ تُبْدُوا ما فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللهُ (Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth belongs to Allah. Whether you disclose what is in your hearts or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it.)

(the Ayah). He said: And the Wilayah (الولاية) had been presented to the Prophets from the time of Adam until Allah sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and it was presented to the nations, but they refused to accept it due to its heaviness (ثقلها). And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted it and presented it to his nation, and they accepted it—the narration. Its entirety has previously appeared in Surat al-Baqarah.

(١١) ما كَذَبَ الْفُؤادُ ما رَأى. (11) The heart did not lie about what it saw.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saw his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. He replied: "Yes, He saw Him with his heart. Have you not heard Allah say: ما كَذَبَ الْفُؤادُ ما رَأى ** (11) The heart did not lie about what it saw. He did not see Him with the sight (بالبصر), but rather He saw Him with the heart (بالفؤاد)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله saw his Lord with his heart (بفؤاده).

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked about this verse, and He replied: "I saw a light (نوراً)."

And in al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about that, and He said: "The heart (فؤاد) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله did not lie about what his eyes saw. Then He informed about what He saw, saying: لَقَدْ رَأى مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِ الْكُبْرى ** (18) He certainly saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord. Thus, the signs (آيات) of Allah are other than Allah."

I say: It has preceded that He saw the greatness (عظمة) of his Lord with his heart (بفؤاده), and the answers only differed due to the differing levels of understanding (افهام) of the addressed parties and the subtlety (غموض) of the matter inquired about.

(١٢) أَفَتُمارُونَهُ عَلى ما يَرى ** (12) Will you dispute with him concerning what he sees? Will you dispute with him concerning it (عليه) from the dispute (المراء)? And it was recited (وقرئ) أفتمرونه (Will you cause him to doubt/dispute), meaning: Will you overcome him (أفتغلبونه) in the dispute (المراء), or will you deny him (أفتجحدونه)? And (على) is used because it implies the meaning of overcoming (الغلبة). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was asked about that revelation (الوحي). He said: "It was revealed to me that ‘Ali is the Master of the Believers (سيّد المؤمنين), the Imam of the Pious (امام المتّقين), the Leader of the Bright-faced (قائد الغرّ المحجّلين), and the first successor (خليفة) whom the Seal of the Prophets (خاتم النبيّين) appoints as successor." Then the people engaged in speech and said: "Is it from Allah or from His Messenger?" Then Allah, majestic is His remembrance (جلّ ذكره), said to His Messenger: "Say to them:" ما كَذَبَ الْفُؤادُ ما رَأى ** (11) The heart did not lie about what it saw. Then He refuted them and said: أَفَتُمارُونَهُ عَلى ما يَرى (Will you dispute with him concerning what he sees?). Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: "I have been commanded concerning him with something other than this; I was commanded to appoint him (أنصبه) for the people and say: 'This is your guardian (وليّكم) after me.' And indeed, he is in the position of the Ark (السّفينة) on the Day of Drowning (يوم الغرق); whoever enters it is saved (نجا), and whoever leaves it is drowned (غرق)."

(١٣) وَلَقَدْ رَآهُ نَزْلَةً أُخْرى (13) and certainly He saw Him in another descent, Another time, with a descent and closeness. (١٤) عِنْدَ سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى (14) near the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary, Which the deeds of the people of the earth terminate at by ascending, as will follow. (١٥) عِنْدَها جَنَّةُ الْمَأْوى (15) near which is the Garden of the Abode. Which the pious (المتّقون) take refuge in.

Al-Qummi: سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى (the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary) is in the seventh heaven, and the Garden of the Abode (جنّة المأوى) is near it.

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): When he was taken on the Night Journey (أُسْرِيَ بِهِ) to the heaven and reached the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary (سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى), a hole was torn for Him in the veils (الحُجُب) the size of a needle's eye (سَمّ الإبرة), and he saw of the Light of Majesty (نور العظمة) what Allah willed Him to see.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When he reached the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary (سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهَى), Gabriel (جبرئيل) stayed behind him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Gabriel, do you abandon me in a place like this?" He replied: "Proceed ahead of you. By Allah, you have reached a station (مَبْلَغاً) that no creature of Allah has reached before you. I saw of the Light of my Lord, and the السّبحة intervened between me and Him." It was asked: "What is the السّبحة?" So he gestured with his face toward the earth and with his hand toward the heaven, saying: "The Majesty of my Lord (جلال ربّي), the Majesty of my Lord (جلال ربّي)," three times.

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): وَلَقَدْ رَآهُ نَزْلَةً أُخْرى عِنْدَ سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى (13) and certainly He saw Him in another descent, (14) near the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary. He means that Gabriel (جبرئيل) met him there when he ascended to the heaven. When he reached the location of the Lote Tree (محلّ السدرة), Gabriel stopped short of it and said: "O Muhammad, this is my station (موقفي) in which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, placed me, and I cannot proceed beyond it. But you, proceed ahead of you to the Lote Tree and stand near it." He said: So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله advanced to the Lote Tree, and Gabriel stayed behind. He said: "It was only named the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary (سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى) because the deeds of the people of the earth ascend with the Guardian Angels (الملائكة الحفظة) to the location of the Lote Tree. And the Noble, Dutiful Guardians (الحفظة الكرام البررة) are short of the Lote Tree; they write down what the angels raise up to them concerning the deeds of the servants on earth." He said: "So they terminate with them at the location of the Lote Tree." He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله looked and saw its branches beneath the Throne (العرش) and around it." He said: "Then the Light of the Almighty (نور الجبّار), the Mighty and Majestic, manifested itself to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. When the Light enveloped Muhammad, his gaze became fixed (شَخَصَ بِبَصَرِهِ) and his joints trembled (ارتعدت فرائصه)." He said: "So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, strengthened the heart of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and fortified his sight until he saw of the signs of his Lord what he saw. And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلَقَدْ رَآهُ نَزْلَةً أُخْرى عِنْدَ سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهى عِنْدَها جَنَّةُ الْمَأْوى (13) and certainly He saw Him in another descent, (14) near the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary, (15) near which is the Garden of the Abode. He means the meeting (الموافاة)." He said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله saw with his sight what he saw of the greatest signs of his Lord (آيات ربّه الكبرى), meaning the greatest of the signs." He (peace be upon him) said: "And the thickness of the Lote Tree is the distance of a hundred years' journey according to the days of this world (أيّام الدنيا), and one leaf of it covers the people of the world."

In al-Majma, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: "I saw upon every leaf of its leaves an angel standing, glorifying Allah, the Most High."

(١٦) إِذْ يَغْشَى السِّدْرَةَ ما يَغْشى ** (16) when there covered the Lote Tree what covered it. This is an exaltation (تعظيم) and magnification (تكثير) of what covered it, such that no description (نعت) can encompass it, nor can any count (عدّ) enumerate it. Al-Qummi said: When the veil (الحجاب) was lifted between Him and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, His Light (نوره) covered the Lote Tree (السّدرة). (١٧) ما زاغَ الْبَصَرُ ** (17) The eye did not swerve, The sight (بصر) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not deviate (ما مال) from what He saw, وَما طَغى (nor did it transgress), meaning, He did not exceed it, but rather He confirmed it with a sound (صحيحاً), upright (مستقيماً) confirmation. (١٨) لَقَدْ رَأى مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِ الْكُبْرى ** (18) He certainly saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord. Meaning, He saw the greatest of the signs (أكبر الآيات), as preceded. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith, He said: And His saying at the end of the verses: ما زاغَ الْبَصَرُ وَما طَغى لَقَدْ رَأى مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِ الْكُبْرى (The eye did not swerve, nor did it transgress. He certainly saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord), He saw Gabriel (جبرئيل) in his form twice: this time and another time. That is because the creation of Gabriel is immense, and he is among the spiritual beings (الروحانيّين) whose creation and description no one comprehends except Allah, the Lord of the worlds (ربّ العالمين). It is said that none of the Prophets saw him in his form except Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم twice: once in the heaven and once on the earth. And al-Qummi says regarding this verse: He certainly heard speech (كلاماً) which, had He not been strong, He would not have been able (to bear). In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: He saw Gabriel, and upon his leg was pearl (الدرّ) like dew (القطر) upon vegetation (البقل). He had six hundred wings (ستّمائة جناح) which filled what is between the heaven and the earth. And al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He said to Ali: "O Ali, Allah made you witness with me in seven instances (مواطن). As for the first of these, it was the night I was taken on the Night Journey (اسري بي) to the heaven. Gabriel said to me: 'Where is your brother?' I said: 'I left him behind me.' He said: 'Call upon Allah to bring him to you.' So I called upon Allah, and behold, your likeness (مثالك) was with me, and behold, the angels (الملائكة) were standing in rows. I said: 'O Gabriel, who are these?' He said: 'They are those with whom Allah boasts about you on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).' So I drew near and spoke concerning what was and what will be until the Day of Resurrection. The second was when I was taken on the Night Journey the second time. Gabriel said to me: 'Where is your brother?' I said: 'I left him behind me.' He said: 'Call upon Allah to bring him to you.' So I called upon Allah, and behold, your likeness was with me. He uncovered for me the seven heavens (سبع سماوات) until I saw their inhabitants (سكّانها), their dwellers (عمّارها), and the place of every angel among them. The third was when I was sent to the Jinn (الجنّ). Gabriel said to me: 'Where is your brother?' I said: 'I left him behind me.' He said: 'Call upon Allah to bring him to you.' So I called upon Allah, and behold, you were with me. I did not say anything to them, nor did they reply anything to me, except that I heard it. The fourth is that we were singled out for the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر), and it is not for anyone other than us. The fifth is that I called upon Allah concerning you, and He gave me everything concerning you except Prophethood (النبوّة), for He said: 'I have singled you out for it and sealed it with you.' As for the sixth, when I was taken on the Night Journey to the heaven, Allah gathered the Prophets (النبيّين) for me, and I led them in prayer, and your likeness was behind me. And the seventh is the destruction of the Confederates (الأحزاب) by our hands." In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "There is no sign (آية) belonging to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), that is greater than me."

(١٩) أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللَّاتَ وَالْعُزَّى. (19) Have you considered al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā? (٢٠) وَمَناةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الْأُخْرى ** (20) and Manāt, the third, the other one? They were idols belonging to them. And al-Lāt was read (وقرئت) with the doubling (بتشديد) of the tā’ (التاء), based on the understanding that it was the image (صورة) of a man who used to mix (يَلُتُّ) sawīq (السويق) with clarified butter (بالسمن) and feed the pilgrims (ويطعم الحاجّ). As for al-ʿUzzā (والعزّى), it is said that its origin is the feminine form (تأنيث) of al-Aʿazz (الاعزّ) (the most mighty/honorable). And Manāt (ومناة) is a faʿlah (فعلة) (form) derived from manāh (مناه) (to cut it) when He cut it (إذا قطعه), for they used to slaughter sacrifices (القرابين) near it. And from it is Minā (منى). And it was read (وقرئ) Man'ah (منأة) based on the understanding that it is a mafʿalah (مفعلة) (form) derived from al-naw’ (النوء) (the setting of a star), as if they were seeking rain (يستمطرون) through the stellar settings (الأنواء) near it, seeking blessing (تبركا) through it. Al-Qummi said: اللَّاتَ ** was a man, and وَالْعُزَّى ** was a woman. And Manāt (وَمَناةَ) was an idol (صنم) on the path (بالمسلك) leading out of the Sacred Precinct (الحرم), six miles away.

(٢١) أَلَكُمُ الذَّكَرُ وَلَهُ الْأُنْثى ** (21) Is the male for you and the female for Him? It is said that this is a denial of what Quraysh claimed: that the angels are the daughters of Allah, and that these idols are either all of them (the angels) or that female jinn (جنيّات) have settled in them, and that these jinn are His daughters—exalted is He far above that. (٢٢) ** تِلْكَ إِذاً قِسْمَةٌ ضِيزى ** (22) That, then, is an unfair division! (It is) unjust (جائرة), since you have assigned to Him that which you yourselves disdain (i.e., the female). And it (ضِيزى) is on the measure of فُعْلَى (فُعْلَى) derived from الضَّيْز (الضَّيْز), which means injustice (الجور). However, its fā' (فاء) (first radical) was vocalized with a kasrah (كُسِر فاؤه) so that the yā' (الياء) might remain sound (ليسلم الياء). And it was recited with the hamzah (بالهمزة) (as ضِئْزَى) from ضَأَزَهُ (ضَأَزَهُ) when he wronged him (إذا ظلمه), on the basis that it is a verbal noun (مصدر) used as an adjective (نعت به). (٢٣) ** إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا أَسْماءٌ ** (23) They are nothing but names The pronoun refers to the idols (الأصنام). That is, in terms of divinity (الألوهيّة), they are nothing but names that you apply to them because you say that they are gods (آلِهة), yet there is nothing in them of the meaning of divinity (الألوهيّة). سَمَّيْتُمُوها أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمْ ** (23) that you and your fathers have named, according to your whims (بهواكم). ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ بِها مِنْ سُلْطانٍ ** (23) for which Allah has sent down no authority. (Meaning) a proof (برهان) upon which you rely. إِنْ يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَ (23) They follow nothing but conjectures, (Meaning) nothing but the illusion (توهّم) that what they are upon is the truth, whether through blind imitation (تقليد) or a false illusion (توهّماً باطلاً). وَما تَهْوَى الْأَنْفُسُ ** (23) and the desires of the soul. And what their souls desire (وما تشتهيه أنفسهم). وَلَقَدْ جاءَهُمْ مِنْ رَبِّهِمُ الْهُدى ** (23) Yet certainly the guidance has come to them from their Lord. (Meaning) the Messenger (الرسول) and the Book (الكتاب), but they abandoned it.

(٢٤)أَمْ لِلْإِنْسانِ ما تَمَنَّى (Does man get whatever he wishes?) The word أم (am) is disjunctive (منقطعة), and the interrogative particle (الهمزة) within it is for denial (للإنكار). The meaning is that not everything he wishes for belongs to him. What is intended is the negation of their greed/hope (طمعهم) for the intercession (شفاعة) of the deities (الآلِهة), and their saying: لَئِنْ رُجِعْتُ إِلى رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِنْدَهُ لَلْحُسْنى (If I am returned to my Lord, I will surely have a good share with Him.) And their saying: لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ (Why was not this Qur’ān sent down to some great man from the two cities?) And similar things.

(٢٥) ** فَلِلَّهِ الْآخِرَةُ وَالْأُولى ** (25) So to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the former [life]. He grants from them whatever He wills to whomever He desires, and it is not for anyone to impose judgment (يَتَحَكَّمَ) upon Him concerning anything from either of them.

(٢٦) ** وَكَمْ مِنْ مَلَكٍ فِي السَّماواتِ لا تُغْنِي شَفاعَتُهُمْ شَيْئاً إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ أَنْ يَأْذَنَ اللهُ ** (26) How many an angel there is in the heavens whose intercession will not avail in any way, except after Allah permits concerning intercession, لِمَنْ يَشاءُ ** (for whomever He wishes)—whether it be from the angels to intercede, or from the people for whom intercession is made—وَيَرْضى ** (and approves). and sees him worthy (أَهْلاً) of that. So how can idols intercede for their worshippers?

(٢٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ لَيُسَمُّونَ الْمَلائِكَةَ تَسْمِيَةَ الْأُنْثى ** (27) Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter name the angels with the naming of females. By naming them daughters. (٢٨) وَما لَهُمْ بِهِ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِنْ يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَّ وَإِنَّ الظَّنَّ لا يُغْنِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ شَيْئاً ** (28) They have no knowledge of that; they follow nothing but conjecture, and conjecture is of no avail against the truth. For the truth, which is the reality (حقيقة) of the thing, is not perceived except through knowledge. (٢٩) فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْ مَنْ تَوَلَّى عَنْ ذِكْرِنا وَلَمْ يُرِدْ إِلَّا الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا ** (29) So disregard him who turns away from Our remembrance and desires nothing but the life of the world. So disregard his invitation (دعوته) and paying attention to his affair (شأنه), for whoever is heedless of Allah and turns away from His remembrance, and plunges into the world such that it is the limit of his concern (همّته) and the extent of his knowledge (مبلغ علمه), the invitation (الدّعوة) will only increase him in obstinacy (عناداً) and persistence (اصراراً) in falsehood.

(٣٠)** ذلِكَ مَبْلَغُهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ** (30) That is the extent of their knowledge. Their knowledge does not go beyond it. The sentence is an interjection confirming the limitation of their aspirations to the worldly life. ** إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ ضَلَّ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ اهْتَدى ** Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are guided. It means that only Allah knows who responds from those who do not respond, so do not tire yourself in inviting them, since nothing is incumbent upon you except the conveyance, and you have already conveyed.

(٣١) وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (31) To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, as creation (خلقاً) and dominion (ملكاً). ** لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَساؤُا بِما عَمِلُوا ** that He may requite those who do evil for what they have done, with the punishment (بعقاب) for the evil (السوء) they have done. وَيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا بِالْحُسْنَى and requite those who do good with the best reward. with the best recompense (بالمثوبة الحُسنى).

(٣٢) الَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبائِرَ الْإِثْمِ ** (32) Those who avoid the major sins That whose punishment is great among the sins, and it is that upon which the specific threat (of punishment) has been arranged, and its explanation has already passed in Surat al-Nisa’. And it was read as كبير الإثم (the great sin), intending the genus or al-shirk (polytheism). وَالْفَواحِشَ ** and indecencies That which is excessive (فَحُشَ) among the major sins, specifically. إِلَّا اللَّمَمَ ** except minor lapses. Except what is little and small, for it is forgiven for those who avoid the major sins, and the exception (الاستثناء) is disconnected (منقطع).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: الْفَواحِشَ ** (the indecencies) are fornication (الزنا) and theft (السرقة), and اللَّمَمَ ** (minor lapses) is the man who commits a slight sin (يُلِمُّ بِالذَّنْبِ) and then seeks forgiveness from Allah for it.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): There is no sin except that a believing servant has been imprinted with it (طُبِعَ عَلَيْهِ). Time passes while he abandons it, then he commits a slight sin (يُلِمُّ بِهِ). This is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: الَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبائِرَ الْإِثْمِ وَالْفَواحِشَ إِلَّا اللَّمَمَ ** (Those who avoid the major sins and indecencies, except minor lapses). He said: Al-Limam (اللّمام) is the servant who commits a slight sin (يُلِمُّ بِالذَّنْبِ) which is not from his innate disposition (سَلِيقَتِهِ), meaning, not from his nature (طَبِيعَة). And in one narration, He said: It is the slight fault (الْهَنَة) after the slight fault, meaning the sin after the sin, which the servant commits a slight sin with (يُلِمُّ بِهِ). And in another (narration), He said: It is the sin which the man commits a slight sin with (يُلِمُّ بِهِ), and he remains (abstaining) for as long as Allah wills, then he commits a slight sin with it again afterwards.

I say: يُلِمُّ بِالذَّنْبِ (commits a slight sin) means he approaches it (يُقَارِبُهُ) and descends to it, so he commits it. And طُبِعَ عَلَيْهِ (has been imprinted with it) means due to an incidental matter (لِعَارِضٍ عَرَضَ لَهُ) that can be removed from him. For this reason, he is able to abandon it. If it were imprinted upon him in the original creation and were part of his character (سَجِيَّتِهِ) and innate disposition (سَلِيقَتِهِ), he would not be able to abandon it. And الْهَنَة is a metaphor for the thing (sin).

إِنَّ رَبَّكَ واسِعُ الْمَغْفِرَةِ ** Indeed your Lord is vast in [His] forgiveness.** Since He forgives the minor sins by avoiding the major sins, and He has the power to forgive whatever He wills of sins, minor or major, for whomever He wills.

هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ ** He knows you best** He is more knowledgeable of your states than you are.

إِذْ أَنْشَأَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ وَإِذْ أَنْتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِي بُطُونِ أُمَّهاتِكُمْ ** since He produced you from the earth, and since you were fetuses in your mothers’ wombs. He knew your states and the outcomes (مَصَارِف) of your affairs when He initiated your creation from the dust and when He fashioned you in the wombs.

فَلا تُزَكُّوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ So do not praise them (تُثْنُوا عَلَيْهَا) with the purity of deeds, the increase of goodness, and purification from disobedience (الْمَعَاصِي) and vices (الرَّذَائِل).

هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ اتَّقى ** He knows best those who are Godwary. For He knows the Godwary (التَّقِيّ) and others among you before He brought you forth from the loins of Adam.

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: He says, let none of you boast about the abundance of his prayer (صَلَاتِهِ), fasting (صِيَامِهِ), zakat (زَكَاتِهِ), and devotion (نُسُكِهِ), because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, knows best who among you is Godwary.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: It is the saying of a person, 'I prayed last night,' and 'I fasted yesterday,' and things similar to this. Then He said: Indeed, there were people who would wake up in the morning and say, 'We prayed last night and we fasted yesterday.' So Ali (peace be upon him) said: "But I sleep night and day, and if I found anything between them, I would sleep it."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Were it not for what Allah has forbidden regarding a person praising his own soul (تَزْكِيَةُ الْمَرْءِ نَفْسَهُ), a speaker would mention numerous virtues which the hearts of the believers recognize and which the ears of the listeners would not reject (تَمُجُّهَا).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether it is permissible for a person to praise his own soul (يُزَكِّيَ الْمَرْءُ نَفْسَهُ). He said: "Yes, if he is compelled to do so. Have you not heard the saying of Joseph: اجْعَلْنِي عَلى خَزائِنِ الْأَرْضِ إِنِّي حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ ** (Put me in charge of the storehouses of the land, for I am a knowing guardian), and the saying of the righteous servant: وَأَنَا لَكُمْ ناصِحٌ أَمِينٌ.** (and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you)?"

(٣٣) أَفَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي تَوَلَّى ** (33) Did you see him who turned away, from following the truth and remaining firm upon it. (٣٤) وَأَعْطى قَلِيلاً وَأَكْدى ** (34) and gave a little, and then stopped? and cut off the giving. In al-Majma', the seven verses—meaning this one and those that follow it—were revealed concerning Uthman ibn Affan. He used to give charity (يَتَصَدَّق) and spend (يُنْفِق). His foster brother, Abdullah ibn Sa'd ibn Abi Sa'id, said to him: "What is this you are doing? Soon nothing will remain for you." Uthman replied: "I have sins, and by what I do, I seek the pleasure (رِضَا) of Allah and hope for His pardon (عَفْو)." Abdullah then said to him: "Give me your she-camel with its saddle, and I will bear all your sins for you." So, he gave it to him, took witnesses against him, and stopped spending (أَمْسَكَ عَنِ النَّفَقَة). Then the verse ** أَفَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي تَوَلَّى ** (Did you see him who turned away) was revealed, meaning on the Day of Uhud when he left the post (الْمَرْكَز), وَأَعْطى قَلِيلاً** (and gave a little), then cut off the spending, up to His saying: ** وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُ سَوْفَ يُرى ** (and that his striving will soon be seen). So, Uthman returned to what he had been doing.

(٣٥) أَعِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْغَيْبِ فَهُوَ يَرى ** (35) Does he possess the knowledge of the Unseen, so that he sees? He knows that his companion bears the burden on his behalf. (٣٦) أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِما فِي صُحُفِ مُوسى. (36) Has he not been informed of what is in the scriptures of Moses, (٣٧) وَإِبْراهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى ** (37) and of Abraham, who fulfilled [his commitments]? He completed and perfected what He was commanded with, and he exerted himself greatly in fulfilling what he committed himself to. Al-Qummi said: He fulfilled what Allah commanded Him with, concerning the command and the prohibition, and the sacrifice of his son.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that He was asked what was meant by His saying: وَإِبْراهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى ** He said: "Words (كلمات) in which he exerted himself greatly." It was asked: "And what are they?" He said: "When he awoke in the morning, he would say: 'I have awoken, and my Lord is Praised. I have awoken, I do not associate anything with Allah, nor do I call upon any god alongside Allah, nor do I take any protector besides Him,' three times. And when he reached the evening, he would say it three times." He said: Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed in His Book: وَإِبْراهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى.**

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): what is similar in meaning.

(٣٨) أَلَّا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ** (38) that no bearer shall bear another’s burden, Meaning, he was not informed of what was in their scriptures (صحفهما) that no one is held accountable for the sin (ذنب) of another. (٣٩) وَأَنْ لَيْسَ لِلْإِنْسانِ إِلَّا ما سَعى ** (39) and that man shall have nothing but what he strives for, Except his striving (سعيه). Meaning, just as no one is held accountable for the sin (ذنب) of another, he is not rewarded for the action (فعله) of another. And what has come in the narrations (الأخبار) that charity (الصدقة) and Hajj (الحجّ) benefit the deceased (الميّت), that is only due to a love (محبّة) which the deceased planted in the heart of the one intending it for him, the one acting on his behalf (النّائب عنه), whether through kindness (إحسان) or faith (ايمان) or kinship (قرابة) or other than that. Thus, it is part of his striving (سعيه). Likewise, the sick person (المريض) is only recorded during the days of his illness (مرضه) with what he used to do during his health (صحّته), because it is in his intention (نيّته) that had he been healthy (صحيحاً), he would have done it. Thus, he is only rewarded for the intention (النيّة), even though the impediment preventing him from doing the action is not in his hands, but rather Allah has overcome him (with illness). Therefore, it is due to the favor (فضل) of Allah that He rewards him.

(40) ** وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُ سَوْفَ يُرى ** (and that his striving will soon be seen) He will see it in the Hereafter. (41) ** ثُمَّ يُجْزاهُ الْجَزاءَ الْأَوْفى ** (Then he will be recompensed with the fullest recompense) Meaning, He recompenses the servant for his striving with the most abundant recompense. (42) ** وَأَنَّ إِلى رَبِّكَ الْمُنْتَهى ** (and that the terminus is toward your Lord) The termination of the creatures and their return.

In al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah says: وَأَنَّ إِلى رَبِّكَ الْمُنْتَهى ** (and that the terminus is toward your Lord). So when the discourse reaches Allah, then refrain (from speaking). And al-Qummi (narrates) the like of it with an addition.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "Indeed, the people before us have excessively engaged in describing (Allah's) attributes (الصفة). What do you say?" He replied: "It is disliked (مكروه). Do you not hear Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: **وَأَنَّ إِلى رَبِّكَ الْمُنْتَهى ** (and that the terminus is toward your Lord)? Speak concerning what is less than that."

(٤٣) وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ أَضْحَكَ وَأَبْكى ** (43) and that it is He who makes [one] laugh and weep, Al-Qummi said: He made the sky weep with rain and made the earth laugh with vegetation. The poet said: "Every day with new chamomile, the earth laughs from the weeping of the sky." (٤٤) وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ أَماتَ وَأَحْيا ** (44) and that it is He who brings death and gives life, None besides Him is capable of causing death and giving life. (٤٥) وَأَنَّهُ خَلَقَ الزَّوْجَيْنِ الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثى. (45) and that He created the two kinds—the male and the female— (٤٦) مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ إِذا تُمْنى ** (46) from a drop of semen when it is emitted, Al-Qummi said: The drop of semen (النطفة) transforms from blood, so it is first blood, then the drop of semen (النطفة) becomes [located] in the brain in a vein called al-Warīd (الوريد). It passes through the vertebrae of the back, and it continues to pass through vertebra after vertebra until it reaches the ureters (الحالبين), whereupon it becomes white. As for the drop of semen (نطفة) of the woman, it descends from her chest.

(٤٧) وَأَنَّ عَلَيْهِ النَّشْأَةَ الْأُخْرى ** (47) and that the second creation is incumbent upon Him, The bringing to life after death, fulfilling His promise. (٤٨) وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ أَغْنى وَأَقْنى ** (48) and that it is He who makes [one] rich and content, And He gave al-qunyah (القنية), which is that which becomes established among possessions (الأموال). In al-Ma'ani and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), regarding this Ayah, He said: "He enriched every human being with his livelihood and made him content with the earning of his hand."

(٤٩) وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ رَبُّ الشِّعْرى ** (49) And that He is the Lord of Sirius. al-Qummi said: It is a star in the sky called al-Shi'ra (الشِّعْرى). Quraysh and a group of the Arabs used to worship it. It is a star that rises at the end of the night.

(٥٠) وَأَنَّهُ أَهْلَكَ عاداً الْأُولى. (50) And that He destroyed the former ‘Ad,

(٥١) وَثَمُودَ ** (51) and Thamud, sparing none of them, And it was recited without tanwin (تنوين) [i.e., وَثَمُودَ instead of وَثَمُودًا]. فَما أَبْقى ** (sparing none of them) refers to the two groups.

(٥٢) وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (52) and the people of Noah before that. Indeed, they were more unjust and more rebellious. وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (and the people of Noah before that) means before ‘Ad and Thamud. إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا هُمْ أَظْلَمَ وَأَطْغى ** (Indeed, they were more unjust and more rebellious) than the two groups, because they used to harm Noah (نوحاً), shun him, and beat him until he was unable to move.

(٥٣) وَالْمُؤْتَفِكَةَ ** (53) And the overturned cities, ** وَالْمُؤْتَفِكَةَ ** (And the overturned cities) refers to the towns (القرى) that were overturned (ائتفكت) with their inhabitants, meaning they were turned upside down (انقلبت). These are the towns of the people of Lot (قوم لوط). أَهْوى ** (He cast down) after He raised them and turned them upside down (قلّبها).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the people of Basra (البصرة); it is al-Mu'tafikah (المؤتفكة).

And al-Qummi said: al-Mu'tafikah (المؤتفكة) is Basra (البصرة). The proof for this is the saying of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "O people of Basra (البصرة), O people of al-Mu'tafikah (المؤتفكة), O army of the woman and followers of the beast! It bellowed, and you answered; it was hamstrung, and you fled. Your water is bitter (زعاق), and your intellects are weak (رقاق). Among you is the seal of hypocrisy (النّفاق), and you have been cursed upon the tongue of seventy prophets. Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله informed me that Gabriel (جبرئيل) informed him that He (Allah) folded the earth for him, and he saw Basra (البصرة) as the closest of the lands to the water and the farthest from the sky. In it are nine-tenths of evil and the chronic disease (الدّاء العضال). The one who resides therein is sinful (مذنب), and the one who leaves it is by mercy. And it has been overturned (ائتفكت) with its inhabitants twice, and upon Allah is the completion of the third time, and the completion of the third time will be in the Return (الرجعة)."

(٥٤) فَغَشَّاها ما غَشَّى ** (54) Then covered it what covered it. The phrase contains an element of terror (تهويل) and generalization (تعميم) regarding what afflicted them. (٥٥) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكَ تَتَمارى ** (55) So which of your Lord’s bounties do you dispute? ** تَتَمارى ** means you doubt (تتشكّك), and the address (الخطاب) is directed to everyone. In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Doubt (الشكّ) rests upon four branches (شعب): suspicion (المرية), caprice (الهوى), hesitation (التردّد), and submission (الاستسلام). And this is the saying of Allah the Almighty: فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكَ تَتَمارى (So which of your Lord’s bounties do you dispute?) It was said that the enumerated things (المعدودات), even if they were blessings (نعماً) and punishments (نقماً), He named them bounties (آلاء) because of the lessons (العبر) and admonitions (المواعظ) contained in His punishments for those who take heed (للمعنبرين), and the vengeance (الانتقام) [He takes] for the Prophets and the believers. And al-Qummi [said]: Meaning, with which authority (بأيّ سلطان) do you dispute (تخاصم)?

(56) ** هذا نَذِيرٌ مِنَ النُّذُرِ الْأُولى.** (56) This is a warning from the warnings of the former [prophets].

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He (peace be upon him) was asked about it, and He said: "Indeed, when Allah the Almighty created the creation in the first particles (الذّرّ الأوّل), He made them stand in rows before Him, and Allah sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, where He called them; so a group believed in Him and a group denied Him. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** هذا نَذِيرٌ مِنَ النُّذُرِ الْأُولى ** (This is a warning from the warnings of the former [prophets]), meaning Muhammad, where He called them to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in the first particles (الذّرّ الأوّل)."

And in al-Basa'ir there is a similar narration.

(٥٧) أَزِفَتِ الْآزِفَةُ ** (57) The Imminent [Hour] has drawn near. Al-Qummi said, meaning: the Resurrection has drawn near. (٥٨) لَيْسَ لَها مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ كاشِفَةٌ ** (58) There is none besides Allah who can avert it. There is no soul capable of averting it except Allah. (٥٩) أَفَمِنْ هذَا الْحَدِيثِ. (59) Do you then wonder at this discourse? In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by "this discourse" (الحديث) what preceded of the reports (الأخبار). [You] wonder (تَعْجَبُونَ) in denial (إنكارا). (٦٠) وَتَضْحَكُونَ ** (60) And laugh, mockingly (استهزاء), وَلا تَبْكُونَ ** and do not weep, in sorrow (تحزّناً) over what you have neglected. (٦١) وَأَنْتُمْ سامِدُونَ ** (61) while you are heedless? Al-Qummi [said], meaning: heedless (لا هون). And it is said: arrogant (مستكبرُون). (٦٢) فَاسْجُدُوا لِلَّهِ وَاعْبُدُوا ** (62) So prostrate to Allah and worship [Him]. meaning: and worship Him, not the deities (الآلهة). In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever persists in reciting Wa al-Najm (والنّجم) every day or every night will live a life praised among the people, and He will be forgiven, and he will be beloved among the people, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).
